justification: All pass criteria met: (1) /health stub identified as 'a lie' that always returns 200 with no dependency checks, (2) consequence clearly named — load balancer and orchestrator cannot detect service failure, keeps routing to broken instances, (3) specific dependency checks recommended — DB connectivity, queue accessibility, SendGrid reachability, with split into /health/live and /health/ready, (4) circuit breaker absence on SendGrid flagged as the 'single biggest operational risk' and ship-blocker, (5) verdict is NOT READY, (6) logging coverage noted as 'adequate' while identifying specific gaps in metrics and tracing.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the health check issue — correctly labels it 'worse than no health endpoint' because it actively misleads the infrastructure layer

Circuit breaker analysis goes beyond identification: quantifies the cascade (3 retries × 10s = 30s thread hold, service self-destructs under load) and specifies concrete thresholds (50% error threshold, 30s window, 60s trip)
Recommends splitting /health into /health/live and /health/ready with specific checks — exceeds the rubric's ask for dependency check recommendations
Identifies additional failure modes beyond rubric scope: 429 rate-limit handling, poison messages, queue backpressure, thundering herd from missing jitter
Operational readiness section provides actionable on-call context — describes a concrete debugging scenario at 3am to illustrate observability gaps
Correctly prioritizes recommendations into ship-blockers vs first-sprint vs nice-to-haves with clear rationale for each tier
